message:
chore(beads): close completed epics and fixed bugs

Close bd-1wgek (duplicate cancel bug already fixed), bd-18a8s
(Reliability Oracles epic, all 6 children done), and bd-2c7u
(Coverage & E2E epic, all 17 children done).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -66,7 +66,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-17uj","title":"bd-ut02: distributed::recovery failure modes","description":"## Unit Tests for src/distributed/recovery.rs (1186 LOC, 16 existing tests)

Existing tests cover basic recovery paths. This bead targets UNTESTED failure modes:

### New Test Cases
- Recovery with duplicate symbols from same replica (ESI collision)
- Recovery with symbols from non-existent/unknown replica ID
- Insufficient symbols (K-1 symbols when K needed) — verify clear error
- Mixed source + repair symbols with exactly K total — boundary
- Symbol hash mismatch during verification — corruption detection
- Recovery cancellation mid-collection (cancel token fires during gather)
- RecoveryCollector ESI deduplication under concurrent insertions
- Recovery checkpoint: interrupted recovery resumes from partial state
- Collector metrics accuracy: verify counts match actual symbol flow
- Recovery with zero replicas configured — immediate failure

### Logging Requirements
Each test logs: replica count, symbols collected, ESIs seen, recovery outcome, timing.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 10+ new tests in recovery.rs #[cfg(test)]
- [ ] All failure modes return typed RecoveryError variants
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified (no resource leaks on cancel)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:16:07.368377675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.167328114Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:54:47.019401457Z","close_reason":"done","deleted_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.167317875Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","distributed","unit-test"]}
 {"id":"bd-17uu","title":"[AA][Sched] Cancel‑lane preemption + fairness","description":"## Goal
Implement cancel‑lane preemption with bounded fairness guarantees for ready/timed lanes.

## Why
Cancellation is latency‑sensitive; it must preempt normal work without starving the system.

## Deliverables
- Priority lane design with strict bound on preemption.
- Fairness proof sketch (no starvation of ready lane).
- Benchmarks for cancel latency.

## Acceptance
- Cancel latency p99 under target budget.
- Ready lane progress guaranteed under worst‑case cancellation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentMoose","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:07:43.000795946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T12:23:47.011359745Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T12:23:47.011227479Z","close_reason":"All deliverables completed in commit 7ebe085 (bd-1tiq): PreemptionMetrics struct, formal fairness proof sketch, 4 tests proving cancel_streak bounded by limit, ready lane guaranteed progress under cancel flood.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17uu","depends_on_id":"bd-1iva","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:07:43.011045492Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-17uu","depends_on_id":"bd-1p8g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:56.830512897Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-17uu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyReef: All deliverables for this bead were completed in commit 7ebe085 (bd-1tiq). Added PreemptionMetrics struct, cancel-lane preemption fairness proof sketch, and 4 tests (test_preemption_metrics_track_dispatches, test_preemption_fairness_yield_under_cancel_flood, test_preemption_max_streak_bounded_by_limit, test_preemption_fallback_cancel_when_only_cancel_work). The cancel_streak_limit mechanism ensures ready lane progress under worst-case cancellation.","created_at":"2026-02-03T12:18:27Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-18mg","title":"WebSocket server/acceptor: upgrade handling and connection management","description":"# WebSocket Server/Acceptor Implementation

## Overview
Implements the WebSocket server/acceptor for inbound connections with full Cx integration,
structured concurrency, and cancel-correct client lifecycle management.

## Background & Motivation
Server-side WebSocket is essential for real-time applications: chat, notifications,
live dashboards, multiplayer games. Must handle many concurrent connections with
proper resource management and cancellation.

## Key Design Points

### WebSocket Acceptor
```rust
pub struct WebSocketAcceptor {
    config: WebSocketConfig,
}

impl WebSocketAcceptor {
    /// Accept a WebSocket upgrade from an HTTP request.
    /// The connection is owned by the region that calls accept().
    pub async fn accept(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        req: &Request,
        stream: TcpStream,
    ) -> Outcome<WebSocket, WsError> {
        // 1. Validate upgrade headers (Connection: Upgrade, Upgrade: websocket)
        // 2. Validate Sec-WebSocket-Key
        // 3. Compute Sec-WebSocket-Accept
        // 4. Send 101 Switching Protocols response
        // 5. Return WebSocket in connected state
    }
}
```

### Connection Lifecycle
- Each accepted connection is owned by its accepting region
- Region cancellation propagates to all child WebSocket connections
- Connections send Close frame (1001) when region closes
- Server can broadcast close to all connections on shutdown

### Integration with HTTP Server
```rust
#[route(GET, "/ws")]
async fn websocket_handler(cx: &Cx, req: Request, stream: TcpStream) -> Response {
    let acceptor = WebSocketAcceptor::new(config);
    let ws = acceptor.accept(cx, &req, stream).await?;
    
    // Spawn WebSocket handler in current region
    cx.spawn(async move |cx| {
        while let Ok(msg) = ws.recv(&cx).await {
            // Handle message
        }
    });
    
    // Return upgrade response (already sent by acceptor)
    Response::switching_protocols()
}
```

### Graceful Shutdown
- Server signals shutdown to all connections via Cx cancellation
- Connections attempt Close handshake with timeout
- After timeout, forcibly drop connections
- No message loss for pending sends

## Dependencies
- Requires: WebSocket frame codec (bd-r4hu)
- Requires: WebSocket handshake (bd-1kg0)
- Blocks: WebSocket E2E tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] accept() validates and completes WebSocket handshake
- [ ] Connections owned by accepting region
- [ ] Cancellation triggers clean close with 1001
- [ ] Integration with HTTP server layer
- [ ] Subprotocol negotiation support
- [ ] Extension negotiation (permessage-deflate)
- [ ] Unit tests for accept/reject paths
- [ ] Integration tests with real HTTP server","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `ws_server::tests::accept_valid_upgrade` - Accept valid WebSocket upgrade
- `ws_server::tests::reject_missing_upgrade_header` - Reject without Upgrade header
- `ws_server::tests::reject_invalid_key` - Reject invalid Sec-WebSocket-Key
- `ws_server::tests::compute_accept_key` - Verify Sec-WebSocket-Accept computation
- `ws_server::tests::subprotocol_negotiation` - Select subprotocol
- `ws_server::tests::extension_negotiation` - Handle extension negotiation
- `ws_server::tests::send_recv_messages` - Basic message exchange
- `ws_server::tests::close_initiated_by_server` - Server initiates close
- `ws_server::tests::close_initiated_by_client` - Handle client close

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- `ws_server::cancel::cancel_during_accept` - Cancel during handshake
- `ws_server::cancel::cancel_active_connection` - Cancel with active connection
- `ws_server::cancel::region_close_all_connections` - Mass close on region close
- `ws_server::cancel::graceful_shutdown` - Ordered shutdown with timeout

### Integration Tests
- `ws_server::integration::accept_multiple_clients` - Multiple concurrent connections
- `ws_server::integration::broadcast_message` - Send to all clients
- `ws_server::integration::client_disconnect_handling` - Handle client drops
- `ws_server::integration::http_server_integration` - With HTTP layer

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Frame details, handshake headers
- DEBUG: Accept/reject decisions, message flow
- INFO: Connection lifecycle (accept, close)
- WARN: Invalid upgrade attempts, protocol violations
- ERROR: Accept failures with full context

### Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync::ws::server=debug,test=info"
cargo test -p asupersync ws_server:: -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee ws_server_tests.log
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:16:58.167942145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:13:34.791662855Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:13:34.791560485Z","close_reason":"Implemented WebSocket server/acceptor with Cx integration: WebSocketAcceptor for upgrade validation, ServerWebSocket for server-side connections, accept()/reject() methods, protocol/extension negotiation. Tests blocked by scheduler errors.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","websocket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-ku6n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-r4hu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-197o","title":"[ALIEN][E2E] CI integration: run Phase 6 end-to-end suites deterministically","description":"## Goal

Wire the Phase 6 end-to-end suites into the standard testing workflow so regressions are caught automatically.

This is intentionally separate from unit tests:
- unit tests validate local invariants
- E2E validates cross-module integration + determinism + artifact stability

## What should run

- GEO e2e (bd-1i1w)
- HOMO e2e (bd-32n6)
- LYAP e2e (bd-zsoh)
- RAPTORQ e2e (bd-15c5)
- PLAN e2e (bd-3gqz)

## CI constraints

- Runs must be deterministic and bounded (no unbounded exploration).
- Output must be stable (prefer artifacts checked by hashes rather than timing).
- Detailed logs should be available in CI artifacts for debugging.

## Acceptance criteria

- A single command (documented in TESTING.md) runs all Phase 6 E2E suites locally.
- CI executes the same command and fails on:
  - invariant violations
  - non-deterministic outputs
  - golden checksum mismatches
- CI stores artifacts (reports + traces) for failed runs for postmortem.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyReef","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:19:29.832727336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T12:02:40.970717222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T12:02:33.079127202Z","close_reason":"CI integration complete. Added phase6-e2e job to .github/workflows/ci.yml running all 5 E2E suites (GEO, HOMO, LYAP, RAPTORQ, PLAN) with artifact upload. Added Phase 6 E2E section to TESTING.md with single-command local runner and suite breakdown table. GEO/HOMO/LYAP are hard gates (47 tests pass). RAPTORQ/PLAN marked continue-on-error due to pre-existing failures (singular matrix, rewrite equivalence).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","ci","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-197o","depends_on_id":"bd-15c5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-197o","depends_on_id":"bd-1i1w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-197o","depends_on_id":"bd-2zce","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-197o","depends_on_id":"bd-32n6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-197o","depends_on_id":"bd-3gqz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-197o","depends_on_id":"bd-zsoh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":153,"issue_id":"bd-197o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyReef: Verified all 5 E2E suites exist and run. Updated TESTING.md with Phase 6 section, suite breakdown, CI expectations. Commit b3b9849.","created_at":"2026-02-03T11:47:00Z"},{"id":154,"issue_id":"bd-197o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CI integration complete. Deliverables: scripts/run_phase6_e2e.sh (local runner), phase6-e2e CI job in ci.yml, TESTING.md docs. 3/5 suites pass (GEO, HOMO, LYAP); RAPTORQ and PLAN have pre-existing failures from concurrent agent work.","created_at":"2026-02-03T12:02:40Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1wgek","title":"[BUG] Local cancel can leave duplicate in local_ready","description":"In three_lane local task routing, cancel paths (inject_cancel / ThreeLaneLocalCancelWaker / schedule_cancel_on_current_local) move tasks to PriorityScheduler cancel lane but do not remove any existing entry in local_ready. If the task was already queued in local_ready, it can be polled again without a wake (extra poll, possible invariant violation).  {"id":"bd-2c2i","title":"Lease manager: time-bounded resource claims","description":"# Lease Manager Implementation

## Goal

Implement a lease system for time-bounded claims on remote resources, preventing orphaned resources when nodes fail.

## Background

In distributed systems, resources can be orphaned when:
1. Client crashes without releasing resource
2. Network partition prevents release message
3. Client hangs indefinitely

Leases solve this: resources are only held for a bounded time. If not renewed, they're automatically released.

## Core Types

### Lease
```rust
pub struct Lease {
    /// Unique lease identifier
    id: LeaseId,
    
    /// Resource being leased
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Holder of the lease
    holder: NodeId,
    
    /// When lease was granted
    granted_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// When lease expires (must renew before)
    expires_at: LogicalTime,
    
    /// Lease state
    state: LeaseState,
}

pub enum LeaseState {
    Active,
    Expiring,  // Close to expiry, renewal needed
    Expired,   // Resource released
    Released,  // Holder explicitly released
}
```

### LeaseId
```rust
pub struct LeaseId {
    /// Resource ID
    resource: ResourceId,
    
    /// Unique grant nonce
    nonce: u64,
    
    /// Granting node
    grantor: NodeId,
}
```

## LeaseManager

```rust
pub struct LeaseManager {
    /// Active leases held by this node
    held: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Leases granted by this node
    granted: HashMap<LeaseId, Lease>,
    
    /// Background renewal task
    renewal_task: TaskHandle,
    
    /// Clock source
    clock: Box<dyn LogicalClock>,
}

impl LeaseManager {
    /// Request a lease on a resource
    async fn acquire(&mut self, resource: ResourceId, duration: Duration) -> Result<Lease, LeaseError>;
    
    /// Renew an existing lease
    async fn renew(&mut self, lease: &mut Lease, duration: Duration) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Release a lease early
    async fn release(&mut self, lease: Lease) -> Result<(), LeaseError>;
    
    /// Check if lease is still valid
    fn is_valid(&self, lease: &Lease) -> bool;
    
    /// Get time until expiry
    fn time_until_expiry(&self, lease: &Lease) -> Duration;
}
```

## Renewal Strategy

```rust
pub struct RenewalConfig {
    /// Renew when this fraction of lease time remains
    renewal_threshold: f64,  // e.g., 0.5 = renew at half-time
    
    /// Retry interval if renewal fails
    retry_interval: Duration,
    
    /// Max retries before giving up
    max_retries: u32,
}
```

Background task renews leases automatically:
1. Monitor all held leases
2. When time_until_expiry < threshold, renew
3. On failure, retry with backoff
4. If max_retries exceeded, trigger lease_lost callback

## Lease Lost Handling

When a lease expires without renewal:
```rust
pub trait LeaseObserver {
    /// Called when lease cannot be renewed
    fn on_lease_lost(&self, lease: LeaseId, reason: LeaseError);
}
```

Default behavior: cancel tasks/actors using the leased resource.

## Clock Considerations

Leases use logical time, not wall time:
- Prevents issues from clock skew
- Lease duration is 'messages' not 'seconds'
- Renewal piggybacks on normal communication

Alternative: Hybrid logical clocks for bounded skew tolerance.

## Wire Protocol

### LeaseRequest
```rust
struct LeaseRequest {
    resource: ResourceId,
    duration: Duration,
    holder: NodeId,
}
```

### LeaseResponse
```rust
enum LeaseResponse {
    Granted { lease: Lease },
    Denied { reason: LeaseDenied },
}

enum LeaseDenied {
    ResourceNotFound,
    AlreadyLeased { holder: NodeId, expires: LogicalTime },
    ResourceLimits,
}
```

## Integration Points

- RemoteHandle holds Lease for remote task
- ActorRef for remote actors has Lease
- Distributed regions use leases for membership
- Saga steps may acquire leases on resources

## Testing

- Lease acquisition and release
- Automatic renewal in background
- Lease expiry when holder crashes
- Multiple lease requests (conflict handling)
- Lab runtime with simulated network delays

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lease and LeaseId types
- [ ] LeaseManager with acquire/renew/release
- [ ] Background renewal task
- [ ] LeaseState transitions
- [ ] LeaseObserver callback
- [ ] Wire protocol messages
- [ ] Logical clock integration
- [ ] Tests for all scenarios","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:22:06.196253260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317154352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:55.317069584Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed","leases","phase4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-1jis","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c2i","depends_on_id":"bd-3sst","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2dbf","title":"HPACK: Huffman invalid padding silently accepted (FIXED)","description":"# Bug Fix: Huffman Invalid Padding (COMPLETED)

## Status: FIXED in session 2026-01-31

## Location
`src/http/h2/hpack.rs` - huffman_decode function

## Vulnerability
The Huffman decoder silently accepted invalid padding (not all 1s) at the end
of encoded data. Per RFC 7541, padding must be the most-significant bits of EOS.
Invalid padding could indicate encoding errors or malicious data.

## Original Code
```rust
// End of decoding - didn't check padding validity
Ok(String::from_utf8(output).map_err(|_| H2Error::compression("invalid UTF-8"))?)
```

## Fix Applied
Validate padding bits are all 1s:

```rust
// After decoding, check remaining bits are valid padding
if bit_offset > 0 {
    // Remaining bits should be all 1s (padding is MSBs of EOS symbol)
    let padding_mask = (1u8 << (8 - bit_offset)) - 1;
    let padding_bits = current_byte & padding_mask;
    if padding_bits != padding_mask {
        return Err(H2Error::compression("invalid Huffman padding"));
    }
}
```

## Testing Required
- test_huffman_decode_invalid_padding
- test_huffman_decode_valid_padding
- Fuzz test: Random byte sequences","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:13.121666105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:31.914317531Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:31.914150501Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2dbf","depends_on_id":"bd-1bic","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2de4","title":"[ALIEN][PLAN] Rewrite rule set + side-condition checker","description":"## Purpose

Encode the lawful rewrites:
- semiring/monoid laws (when declared by policy)
- join/race/timeout algebraic rewrites
- cancellation/obligation laws (loser draining, finalize ordering)

## Deliverables

- Each rule is defined as:
  - pattern -> replacement
  - side conditions (checked)
  - explanation text (for galaxy-brain)

## Acceptance criteria

- Rules are deterministic and only fire when conditions hold.
- Unit tests cover each rule with a small witness plan.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:22.783688540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:39:18.771355282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:39:18.771199602Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","plan","rewrites"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2de4","depends_on_id":"bd-1cac","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2de4","depends_on_id":"bd-1cir","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2de4","depends_on_id":"bd-26v8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
